name: Vector Databases
scope:
A person with BASIC proficiency level in Vector Databases as a competency should be able to / is capable of explaining vector vs relational / document stores, high-dimensional embeddings, dimension consistency, cosine–dot-product–Euclidean metrics, ANN search behaviour and trade-offs, and index families HNSW, IVF-Flat, PQ and disk-based variants.  
They can run an ingestion pipeline that chooses a text / image / audio embedding model (e.g., OpenAI, Sentence-Transformers, CLIP), performs scaling / normalisation, attaches structured metadata, batches or streams idempotent inserts/ upserts and copes with eventual consistency.  
They create collections with correct parameters (dimension, metric, efConstruction, nlist, M, efSearch, nprobe), and execute CRUD plus k-NN, hybrid Boolean+vector, range or paginated searches.  
They evaluate retrieval quality with precision@k, recall@k, MRR, NDCG and adjust index knobs to hit agreed recall-latency targets.  
They wire the engine (Milvus, Faiss, Weaviate, Qdrant, Pinecone, etc.) into services through Python/JS SDKs, REST or gRPC, powering semantic-search or RAG modules and handling connection pooling, retries and back-pressure.  
They configure replication factors, WAL/durability modes, snapshots, automated backups, monitor memory, index-build progress, query latency and resolve issues like dimension mismatch, memory exhaustion or degraded recall.  
They can scale from ~1 M to ~10 M vectors by sharding or adding nodes, understanding memory-to-latency cost curves and when to switch to disk-ANN or hybrid indexes.  
They enable TLS, API-keys or token auth, collection-level RBAC, encrypted backups and participate in access reviews and data-retention audits.  
They estimate RAM / storage footprints given dimension and quantisation, run load tests, and produce cost-performance reports that recommend efSearch/ nprobe tuning or partial index rebuilds.  
They routinely employ Docker/K8s, CI pipelines and vendor CLIs or dashboards for deployment and ops while knowing they are not yet implementing custom codecs or novel index algorithms.  
They document schemas, ingestion scripts, runbooks and PR rationales so peers can reproduce work, and can demo a stable semantic-search feature sustaining double-digit QPS with <150 ms P95 latency and ≈0.85 recall.
